instruction: Implement a complete Axum web service with tower middleware for authentication, request tracing, and rate limiting with typed error responses.
output:
<think>
This requires careful architectural thinking. Let me analyze the key design decisions and trade-offs involved. I need to consider production-readiness, error handling, scalability, and maintainability. The implementation should demonstrate real-world patterns with proper separation of concerns. I should think about edge cases, performance implications, and how components interact. Let me start with the core abstractions and build up to a complete, working implementation that handles the complexities of real systems.
</think>

```rust
use axum::{Router, routing::{get, post}, extract::State, Json, http::StatusCode, response::IntoResponse};
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use std::sync::Arc;
use tokio::sync::RwLock;

#[derive(Clone)]
struct AppState { db: Arc<RwLock<Vec<Item>>> }

#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize, Clone)]
struct Item { id: String, name: String, value: f64 }

#[derive(Serialize)]
struct ErrorResponse { error: String, code: String }

enum AppError { NotFound(String), Validation(String), Internal(String) }

impl IntoResponse for AppError {
    fn into_response(self) -> axum::response::Response {
        let (status, msg) = match self {
            AppError::NotFound(m) => (StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, m),
            AppError::Validation(m) => (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, m),
            AppError::Internal(m) => (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, m),
        };
        (status, Json(ErrorResponse { error: msg, code: status.to_string() })).into_response()
    }
}

async fn list_items(State(state): State<AppState>) -> impl IntoResponse {
    let items = state.db.read().await;
    Json(items.clone())
}

async fn create_item(
    State(state): State<AppState>,
    Json(item): Json<Item>,
) -> Result<impl IntoResponse, AppError> {
    if item.name.is_empty() {
        return Err(AppError::Validation("Name required".into()));
    }
    state.db.write().await.push(item.clone());
    Ok((StatusCode::CREATED, Json(item)))
}

fn app() -> Router {
    let state = AppState { db: Arc::new(RwLock::new(Vec::new())) };
    Router::new()
        .route("/items", get(list_items).post(create_item))
        .with_state(state)
}
```

This Axum service demonstrates typed error handling via IntoResponse, shared state with RwLock for concurrent access, and clean routing with extract-based dependency injection. Errors automatically convert to appropriate HTTP status codes with consistent JSON error bodies.
